description:
You are a QA engineer who must prepare a small, self-contained text-encoding regression suite in /home/user/encoding_tests. The automated grader will verify your work strictly, so follow the exact filenames, encodings, and logging format shown below. 1. Create the working directory • /home/user/encoding_tests 2. Create the two _source_ text files **exactly** as described: a. utf8_spanish.txt - Location: /home/user/encoding_tests/utf8_spanish.txt - Encoding: UTF-8 - Human-readable text (one trailing newline): 
Español: ¿Cómo estás?
b. iso_french.txt - Location: /home/user/encoding_tests/iso_french.txt - Encoding: ISO-8859-1 - Human-readable text (one trailing newline): 
Français: Ça va?
3. Produce two _converted_ files by swapping their encodings: • Convert utf8_spanish.txt ➜ ISO-8859-1 → output file name **utf8_spanish_iso.txt** in the same directory. • Convert iso_french.txt ➜ UTF-8 → output file name **iso_french_utf8.txt** in the same directory. The converted files must contain **exactly** the same visible characters shown in the source files (including the final newline), only their byte encoding changes. 4. Create a single text log named /home/user/encoding_tests/conversion_results.log. • The log has precisely two lines — one per conversion. • Each line is a colon-separated, four-field record with **no extra spaces**: 
ORIGINAL_FILE:TARGET_FILE:TARGET_ENCODING:STATUS
For this task • ORIGINAL_FILE is the basename of the file you started with • TARGET_FILE is the basename of the newly created file • TARGET_ENCODING is either ISO-8859-1 or UTF-8 • STATUS must be the literal string OK if the conversion succeeded, otherwise FAIL (the grader expects both to be OK) Example layout (you must of course use the filenames that apply here): 
utf8_spanish.txt:utf8_spanish_iso.txt:ISO-8859-1:OK
iso_french.txt:iso_french_utf8.txt:UTF-8:OK
5. When everything is finished, the directory must contain **exactly five items**: the two original files, the two converted files, and the log file. Notes & constraints • Use only tools that are available in a standard Linux user shell (e.g. iconv, file, iconv -f/​-t, shell redirection, etc.). • Do NOT remove or rename any of the required files. • The grader will check: – existence of all files in the correct paths – correct number of lines and exact colon-separated layout in the log – that the converted files’ byte encodings match the requested targets and that their visible text is unchanged. • The filenames, text, and log layout are case-sensitive. • No root privileges are available; keep everything inside /home/user/encoding_tests. Complete the task entirely from a Bash terminal session.
truth:
EXPECT_DIR=/home/user/encoding_tests

# 1. Exact byte sequence (hex) that must be inside the converted Spanish file (ISO-8859-1)
SPANISH_ISO_FILE=${EXPECT_DIR}/utf8_spanish_iso.txt
SPANISH_ISO_HEX=45737061f16f6c3a20bf43f36d6f20657374e1733f0a

# 2. Exact byte sequence (hex) that must be inside the converted French file (UTF-8)
FRENCH_UTF8_FILE=${EXPECT_DIR}/iso_french_utf8.txt
FRENCH_UTF8_HEX=4672616ec3a76169733a20c387612076613f0a

# 3. Log file structure
LOG_FILE=${EXPECT_DIR}/conversion_results.log
LOG_EXPECTED_LINES=2
LOG_REGEX_1=^utf8_spanish\.txt:utf8_spanish_iso\.txt:ISO-8859-1:OK$
LOG_REGEX_2=^iso_french\.txt:iso_french_utf8\.txt:UTF-8:OK$

# 4. Directory listing must contain exactly:
#    utf8_spanish.txt
#    iso_french.txt
#    utf8_spanish_iso.txt
#    iso_french_utf8.txt
#    conversion_results.log
name: task_000000_b34bde0f
